reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the Goodhart's law dynamic, note that activated users are roughly flat, recommend paying on activation rather than signup, suggest segmenting referred vs. organic activation to rule out confounders, and propose fraud/farming safeguards plus tracking cost-per-activated-user instead of cost-per-signup. Neither contains a factual or reasoning error. The two responses are nearly identical in structure and content — B adds a closing note that signups will likely drop post-fix (a minor expectation-setting remark, not a new decision-relevant fact) and A adds a specific double-bonus mechanic slightly earlier, but neither difference would meaningfully change what the asker does next. Length and phrasing differ but substance and actionability are equivalent.
